examples.title: Basic Frame Buffer Initialization
examples.description: Demonstrates how to allocate and initialize a frame buffer for display
examples.code:
// Frame Buffer Initialization Algorithm
BEGIN
 SET width = 1920
 SET height = 1080
 SET colorDepth = 32 // bits per pixel
 
 // Calculate buffer size
 SET bytesPerPixel = colorDepth / 8
 SET bufferSize = width * height * bytesPerPixel
 
 // Allocate frame buffer in VRAM
 ALLOCATE frameBuffer[bufferSize]
 
 // Initialize to black (0x000000)
 FOR y = 0 TO height-1 DO
 FOR x = 0 TO width-1 DO
 SET pixelIndex = (y * width + x) * bytesPerPixel
 frameBuffer[pixelIndex] = 0 // Blue
 frameBuffer[pixelIndex+1] = 0 // Green
 frameBuffer[pixelIndex+2] = 0 // Red
 frameBuffer[pixelIndex+3] = 255 // Alpha
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 RETURN frameBuffer
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm allocates memory for a 1920×1080 frame buffer with 32-bit color (RGBA) and initializes all pixels to black.

examples.title: Double Buffering Swap Algorithm
examples.description: Shows how double buffering swaps front and back buffers to prevent flicker
examples.code:
// Double Buffering Algorithm
BEGIN
 // Initialize two buffers
 ALLOCATE frontBuffer
 ALLOCATE backBuffer
 
 WHILE applicationRunning DO
 // Draw new frame to back buffer
 CLEAR backBuffer
 
 FOR each object IN scene DO
 RENDER object TO backBuffer
 END FOR
 
 // Wait for vertical blank (display refresh)
 WAIT_FOR_VSYNC()
 
 // Swap buffers
 TEMP = frontBuffer
 frontBuffer = backBuffer
 backBuffer = TEMP
 
 // Display controller reads from front buffer
 DISPLAY_CONTROLLER.setSource(frontBuffer)
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Double buffering prevents visual artifacts by drawing to an off-screen buffer and swapping during vertical blanking interval.

examples.title: Z-Buffer Algorithm for Hidden Surface Removal
examples.description: Implements depth testing to determine visible surfaces in 3D rendering
examples.code:
// Z-Buffer Algorithm
BEGIN
 SET width = 800
 SET height = 600
 
 // Allocate color and depth buffers
 ALLOCATE colorBuffer[width][height]
 ALLOCATE zBuffer[width][height]
 
 // Initialize depth buffer to maximum (far plane)
 FOR y = 0 TO height-1 DO
 FOR x = 0 TO width-1 DO
 zBuffer[x][y] = INFINITY
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 // Process each triangle in scene
 FOR each triangle IN triangles DO
 // Rasterize triangle to fragments
 FOR each fragment IN rasterize(triangle) DO
 x = fragment.x
 y = fragment.y
 z = fragment.depth
 
 // Depth test: only draw if closer
 IF z < zBuffer[x][y] THEN
 zBuffer[x][y] = z
 colorBuffer[x][y] = fragment.color
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 RETURN colorBuffer
END
examples.explanation: The Z-buffer algorithm stores depth for each pixel and only updates pixels that are closer than previously rendered surfaces.
